BulletinMeasurements.highlights: Natural avalanches are to be expected.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: For those venturing off piste a dangerous avalanche situation will be encountered over a wide area.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
The large quantity of fresh snow and the sometimes large wind slabs that are forming in all aspects can be released easily or naturally above the tree line. Avalanches can be triggered in near-surface layers and reach medium size. Additionally in some places avalanches can penetrate near-ground layers of the snowpack and reach large size on west, north and east facing slopes, especially above approximately 2200 m.
The danger exists primarily in alpine snow sports terrain.

In addition mostly small natural avalanches are possible, especially on extremely steep slopes.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
40 to 60 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, will fall. The wind will be storm force. The wind will transport the new snow significantly. Deep wind slabs will form.

The new snow and wind slabs will be deposited on the unfavourable surface of an old snowpack above the tree line. The old snowpack will be weakly bonded, especially on west, north and east facing slopes above approximately 2200 m, and on south facing slopes in high Alpine regions.
BulletinMeasurements.tendencyComment:
As a consequence of a moderate to strong wind from northerly directions, further wind slabs will form.

Avalanches can as before be released easily.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights:
Wind and new snow: Wind slabs are to be evaluated with care and prudence.
The avalanche prone locations are to be found in particular in gullies and bowls, and behind abrupt changes in the terrain at intermediate and high altitudes.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
Some snow will fall until the early morning in particular along the border with France and along the border with Switzerland. The northwesterly wind will transport the fresh and old snow significantly.
As a consequence of low temperatures, snowfall and the strong wind, the snow drift accumulations will increase in size.
Adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls dry slab avalanches are possible, even quite large ones.
Avalanches can be released, even by a single winter sport participant or triggered naturally, caution is to be exercised in particular on wind-loaded slopes, and at transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack, when entering gullies and bowls for example.

Backcountry touring calls for caution and restraint.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
Especially along the border with France and along the border with Switzerland 10 to 15 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, will fall until the early morning.
The foehn wind will transport the fresh and old snow significantly.
Over a wide area new snow and wind slabs are lying on a hard crust.

As a consequence of low temperatures snowfall and the moderate to strong northwesterly wind, the snow drift accumulations will increase in size. The weather conditions will give rise to unfavourable bonding of the snowpack in particular at intermediate and high altitudes.

In particular adjacent to ridgelines at high altitude snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind.
BulletinMeasurements.tendencyComment: The weather will be cold. Strong foehn wind: By Friday further wind slabs will form in gullies and bowls and behind abrupt changes in the terrain as well as above the tree line.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights:
Stormy weather and new snow.
A sometimes precarious avalanche situation will prevail.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
The storm force wind will transport the fresh and old snow significantly. As a consequence of low temperatures, snowfall and the strong wind, the snow drift accumulations will increase in size. Adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls dry slab avalanches are possible, even quite large ones. Avalanches can be released, even by a single winter sport participant or triggered naturally, caution is to be exercised in particular on wind-loaded slopes, and at transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack, when entering gullies and bowls for example.

Weak layers exist in the snowpack on east, north and west facing slopes. Weak layers in the old snowpack can be released in some places by individual winter sport participants especially here. Avalanches can in isolated cases be triggered in the old snowpack and reach quite a large size.

In addition there is a danger of loose snow avalanches.

There is a danger of falling on the hard crust. Backcountry touring calls for caution and restraint.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
15 to 25 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, will fall. The wind will be storm force. The northerly wind will transport the new snow significantly. Extensive wind slabs will form.

The new snow and wind slabs will be deposited on the unfavourable surface of an old snowpack above the tree line. The old snowpack will be weakly bonded, especially on west, north and east facing slopes above approximately 2200 m, and on south facing slopes at elevated altitudes.
BulletinMeasurements.tendencyComment: As a consequence of stormy weather a considerable avalanche danger will persist. The avalanches can in some places be released easily.

BulletinMeasurements.highlights: Natural avalanches are to be expected.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: For those venturing off piste a dangerous avalanche situation will be encountered over a wide area.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
The large quantity of fresh snow and the sometimes large wind slabs that are forming in all aspects can be released easily or naturally above the tree line. Avalanches can be triggered in near-surface layers and reach medium size. Additionally in some places avalanches can penetrate near-ground layers of the snowpack and reach large size on west, north and east facing slopes, especially above approximately 2200 m.
The danger exists primarily in alpine snow sports terrain.

In addition mostly small natural avalanches are possible, especially on extremely steep slopes.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
25 to 50 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, will fall. The wind will be strong to storm force. The wind will transport the new snow significantly. Deep wind slabs will form.

The new snow and wind slabs will be deposited on the unfavourable surface of an old snowpack above the tree line. The old snowpack will be weakly bonded, especially on west, north and east facing slopes above approximately 2200 m, and on south facing slopes in high Alpine regions.
BulletinMeasurements.tendencyComment:
As a consequence of a moderate to strong wind from northerly directions, further wind slabs will form.

Avalanches can as before be released easily.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: Increase in danger of dry avalanches as a consequence of new snow and wind.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
As a consequence of sharply falling temperatures, snowfall and the occasionally storm force northwesterly wind, a treacherous avalanche situation will develop.
Wednesday: The prevalence of avalanche prone locations and likelihood of triggering will increase during the course of the night.
These conditions will bring about a rise in the danger of dry avalanches. Also at a distance from ridgelines occasionally large dry slab avalanches are possible.

Along the border with France and along the border with Switzerland the avalanche prone locations are more prevalent. Especially wind-loaded slopes: The fresh snow as well as all the wind slabs can be released by a single winter sport participant. Backcountry touring calls for caution and restraint.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
20 to 30 cm of snow, and up to 50 cm in some localities, will fall until the evening in all altitude zones. Especially in the northern areas bordering France and Switzerland.
Towards its surface, the snowpack is soft and has a loosely bonded surface.
The wind will transport the snow. The fresh wind slabs are bonding poorly with the old snowpack.
Weak layers exist in the snowpack in particular on steep shady slopes.
Especially adjacent to ridgelines in all aspects: Over a wide area snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind.
BulletinMeasurements.tendencyComment: The weather will be cold. The avalanche danger will persist.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights:
Increase in avalanche danger as a consequence of new snow and strong wind.
The avalanche prone locations are to be found in particular in gullies and bowls, and behind abrupt changes in the terrain at intermediate and high altitudes.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment: The storm force wind will transport the fresh and old snow significantly. As a consequence of low temperatures, snowfall and the strong wind, the snow drift accumulations will increase in size. Adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls dry slab avalanches are possible, even quite large ones. Avalanches can be released, even by a single winter sport participant or triggered naturally. Weak layers exist in the old snowpack on northwest, north and northeast facing slopes. Avalanches can be triggered in the old snowpack reach large size in particular on northwest, north and northeast facing slopes.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
The small quantity of fresh snow and in particular the wind slabs formed by the strong wind are lying on the unfavourable surface of an old snowpack.
Individual weak layers exist in the old snowpack especially on shady slopes. Avalanches can in some places be released in deep layers in particular on northwest, north and northeast facing slopes.
Sunshine and high temperatures will give rise as the day progresses to moistening of the snowpack in particular on sunny slopes.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: Increase in avalanche danger as a consequence of new snow and strong wind.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment: As a consequence of the precipitation the prevalence and size of the avalanche prone locations will increase. The fresh wind slabs are bonding poorly with the old snowpack. They represent the main danger. The avalanche prone locations are to be found in particular at the base of rock walls, and adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls. In particular in the regions exposed to heavier precipitation the avalanches can be released naturally and reach very large size in some cases.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment: As a consequence of new snow and strong wind the wind slabs will increase in size additionally. The expected fresh snow, which will be heavier in the eastern parts of our region, accompanied by strong winds from the north, will form a snowpack that, especially on sunny slopes, will have a hard surface crust in many places. On shaded slopes, however, in addition to the old weak basal layers, the presence of granular snow and frost crystals has been reported in the surface layers.
BulletinMeasurements.tendencyComment:
As a consequence of the strong wind the wind slabs will increase in size additionally.
The weather will be very cloudy over a wide area.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: Gradual increase in danger of dry avalanches as a consequence of the strong wind.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
As a consequence of sharply falling temperatures and the strong to storm force northwesterly wind, a treacherous avalanche situation will develop.
Wednesday: The prevalence of avalanche prone locations and likelihood of triggering will increase during the course of the night.
These conditions will bring about a rise in the danger of dry avalanches. Also at a distance from ridgelines medium-sized and, in isolated cases, large dry slab avalanches are possible.
Especially wind-loaded slopes: The fresh snow as well as all the wind slabs can be released by a single winter sport participant.
Backcountry touring calls for caution and restraint.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
10 to 20 cm of snow, but less in some localities, will fall until the evening in all altitude zones. The wind will transport the fresh and old snow. The fresh wind slabs are bonding poorly with the old snowpack.
Towards its surface, the snowpack is soft and has a loosely bonded surface.

Weak layers exist in the snowpack in particular on steep shady slopes.
Especially adjacent to ridgelines in all aspects: Over a wide area snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind.
BulletinMeasurements.tendencyComment: The weather will be cold. The avalanche danger will persist.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: A sometimes precarious avalanche situation will prevail.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
Wind slabs are very prone to triggering in all aspects above the tree line. These can be released in near-surface layers. Avalanches can also penetrate near-ground layers of the snowpack and reach medium size on west, north and east facing slopes.
Natural avalanches are to be expected. This applies especially at the base of rock walls and behind abrupt changes in the terrain.

In addition an appreciable danger of loose snow avalanches exists. Gliding avalanches can also occur, especially on cut and grassy slopes.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
20 to 30 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, will fall. The wind will be storm force. The northwesterly wind will transport the new snow significantly. Extensive wind slabs will form.

The new snow and wind slabs will be deposited on the unfavourable surface of an old snowpack above the tree line. The old snowpack will be weakly bonded, especially on west, north and east facing slopes above approximately 2200 m, and on south facing slopes at elevated altitudes.
BulletinMeasurements.tendencyComment:
As a consequence of a sometimes storm force wind from northerly directions, further wind slabs will form on Friday.

Avalanches can be released easily.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: New snow and wind slabs in all aspects. New snow represents the main danger.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment: As a consequence of new snow and a strong to storm force northwesterly wind, sometimes deep wind slabs will form in the course of the day in all aspects. In all aspects individual small and, in isolated cases, medium-sized slab avalanches are to be expected as the snowfall becomes more intense. In particular on wind-loaded slopes the avalanches can be released in deep layers of the snowpack and reach medium size in isolated cases.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment: The old snowpack is largely stable. The new snow will be deposited on a quite favourable old snowpack in particular on shady slopes.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: A sometimes precarious avalanche situation will prevail.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
Wind slabs are very prone to triggering in all aspects above the tree line. These can be released in near-surface layers. Avalanches can also penetrate near-ground layers of the snowpack and reach medium size on west, north and east facing slopes.
Natural avalanches are to be expected. This applies especially at the base of rock walls and behind abrupt changes in the terrain.

In addition an appreciable danger of loose snow avalanches exists.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
5 to 15 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, will fall. The wind will be storm force. The northwesterly wind will transport the fresh and old snow significantly. Extensive wind slabs will form.

The new snow and wind slabs will be deposited on the unfavourable surface of an old snowpack above the tree line. The old snowpack will be weakly bonded, especially on west, north and east facing slopes above approximately 2200 m, and on south facing slopes at elevated altitudes.
BulletinMeasurements.tendencyComment:
As a consequence of a sometimes storm force wind from northerly directions, further wind slabs will form on Friday.

Avalanches can be released easily.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: Fresh wind slabs are to be evaluated with care and prudence.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
Wind slabs are very prone to triggering in particular on very steep shady slopes above the tree line. These can be released in near-surface layers. Avalanches can in very isolated cases penetrate near-ground layers of the snowpack and reach medium size on west, north and east facing slopes.
Individual natural avalanches are not ruled out.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment: 5 to 15 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, will fall. The wind will be storm force. The northwesterly wind will transport the new snow. Avalanche prone wind slabs will form.
BulletinMeasurements.tendencyComment: As a consequence of a sometimes storm force wind from northerly directions, further wind slabs will form on Friday.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: Wind slabs require caution, in the regions exposed to a lot of wind in particular along the border with France.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
As a consequence of a moderate to strong wind from northwesterly directions, wind slabs formed in the course of the day in particular above the tree line.
In particular in gullies and bowls avalanches can be released in the various wind slab layers and reach medium size. Individual medium-sized to large avalanches are, however, not ruled out.

Wind slabs can in isolated cases be released, even by a single winter sport participant. This applies in particular at transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack, when entering gullies and bowls for example, and on steep slopes.
Off-piste activities call for caution.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
The northwesterly wind has transported the loosely bonded old snow.
As a consequence of sharply falling temperatures and the moderate to strong foehn wind, snow drift accumulations formed.
High altitudes and the high Alpine regions: Wind slabs are lying on the smooth surface of an old snowpack.

In particular in the vicinity of peaks snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind.
BulletinMeasurements.tendencyComment: The weather will be cold. Along the border with France and at elevated altitudes the avalanche prone locations are more prevalent and the danger is greater.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: Wind slabs and wet snow represent the main danger.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
As a consequence of new snow and wind individual small to medium-sized slab avalanches are possible above approximately 1300 m.
In the vicinity of peaks a dangerous avalanche situation will be encountered in some localities. The more recent wind slabs and can be released easily.
Avalanches can penetrate down to the ground and reach medium size in some cases.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
The small quantity of fresh snow and in particular the wind slabs formed by the strong wind are lying on the unfavourable surface of an old snowpack.
Sunshine and high temperatures will give rise as the day progresses to moistening of the snowpack in particular on sunny slopes.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: New snow and wind slabs in the early morning. The new snow represents the main danger.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment: As a consequence of new snow and a sometimes storm force wind, sometimes avalanche prone wind slabs will form in the course of the day in all aspects. In particular in northeast to east to southwest facing aspects individual mostly small slab avalanches are possible as the snowfall becomes more intense.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment: The new snow will be deposited on the quite favourable surface of an old snowpack in particular on shady slopes.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: Wet snow represents the main danger.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment: As the day progresses as a consequence of warming during the day there will be an increase in the danger of wet avalanches. Above approximately 1300 m small moist and wet avalanches are possible.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment: The snowpack will be in most cases moist.
